decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was delivered last Friday, and no P0 bugs were identified.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry duration is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed upon in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails are not being rate-limited
decisions.detail: There is a current issue where password reset emails are not subject to rate limiting.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Maximum 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed during the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway using AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min limit per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway, enforcing a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec publication
decisions.detail: The mobile app team is currently unable to proceed until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for auth API spec: 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is July 5, 2026.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for all three auth endpoints
decisions.detail: All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented before the Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: An open question remains regarding the system's fallback behavior in the event of an API gateway failure.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Invalidation of refresh tokens on password reset
decisions.detail: An open question exists regarding whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user resets their password.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate limiting for password reset emails, adhering to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: Maximum 3 password reset attempts per hour must be enforced.
The API gateway must enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: JWT token expiry duration is set to 24 hours.
The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior if the API gateway goes down?
Are refresh tokens invalidated when a user resets their password?

acceptance_criteria: Given a JWT access token is issued, When the current time exceeds 24 hours from issuance, Then the JWT access token must be considered invalid.
Given a user attempts a password reset, When the user has made 3 or fewer password reset requests within a 60-minute window, Then a password reset email is successfully sent for each valid request.

Given a user attempts a password reset, When the user has made more than 3 password reset requests within a 60-minute window, Then subsequent requests within that window must be rejected, and no further password reset email is sent.
Given a user makes API requests, When the user makes 100 or fewer requests within a 60-second window, Then all requests are processed successfully.
Given a user makes API requests, When the user makes more than 100 requests within a 60-second window, Then subsequent requests from that user within that window must be throttled or rejected.
Given valid user credentials, When a user attempts to log in, Then a valid JWT access token and refresh token are issued, and a successful login response is returned.
Given invalid user credentials, When a user attempts to log in, Then an error response indicating invalid credentials is returned, and no tokens are issued.
Given a valid and non-expired refresh token, When a user requests to refresh their tokens, Then a new valid JWT access token and refresh token are issued, and a successful response is returned.
Given an invalid or expired refresh token, When a user requests to refresh their tokens, Then an error response is returned, and no new tokens are issued.

Given a request to initiate a password reset for a registered email address, When the password reset rate limit is not exceeded, Then a password reset email is sent, and a generic success message is returned to the user.

Given a request to initiate a password reset for an unregistered email address, When the password reset rate limit is not exceeded, Then a generic success message is returned to the user, and no password reset email is sent.
test_scenarios: Verify JWT token is valid immediately after issuance.
Verify JWT token is valid at 23 hours 59 minutes after issuance.
Verify JWT token is invalid at 24 hours 1 minute after issuance.
Perform 3 password reset requests for the same user within 59 minutes and verify all requests are successful (emails sent).
Perform 4 password reset requests for the same user within 59 minutes and verify the 4th request is blocked (no email sent).
Perform 3 password reset requests, wait 61 minutes, then perform another 3 password reset requests, verifying all 6 requests over the total period succeed.
Perform 100 API requests for a single user within 50 seconds and verify all requests are successful.
Perform 101 API requests for a single user within 50 seconds and verify the 101st request receives a throttling error (e.g., 429 Too Many Requests).
Log in with valid credentials and verify a successful response containing both access and refresh tokens.
Attempt to log in with a correct username and an incorrect password, verifying authentication fails with an appropriate error.
Attempt to log in with an unregistered username, verifying authentication fails with an appropriate error (e.g., invalid credentials, not user not found).
Use a valid, non-expired refresh token to obtain a new pair of access and refresh tokens, verifying the new tokens are issued.
Use an expired refresh token to obtain new tokens, verifying the refresh request fails with an appropriate error.
Initiate a password reset for a valid user email and confirm the password reset email is sent (observing D004 rate limit).
Initiate a password reset for an email not registered in the system, confirming a generic success message is returned but no email is sent.
edge_cases: Test JWT token validity exactly at the 24-hour mark (considering potential time synchronization differences).
Test concurrent password reset requests from the same user across multiple sessions or devices.
Test password reset requests made exactly at the 60-minute boundary to reset the rate limit.
Test API Gateway throttling with a burst of 100 requests in a very short period (e.g., 1 second).
Test API Gateway throttling for a user making 100 requests in the first 5 seconds of a minute, then pausing until the next minute.
Test login attempts with special characters or maximum length values for username/password fields.
Test refresh token usage immediately prior to its natural expiry.
unresolved_behaviors: Clarify whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user successfully resets their password. This affects user session management and security post-reset.

decision_history: Auth module was successfully delivered last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
Agreed that JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours (reconfirmed from Sprint 2 decision).
Security review decision (2026-06-28): Implement a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.
Confirmed API gateway implementation using AWS API Gateway, enforcing a 100 requests/minute/user limit.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) require automated integration tests prior to Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked by the publication of the Authentication API specification.
Definition of fallback behavior if API gateway goes down is needed (owned by System Architect/Engineer) to ensure system resilience.
Clarification on whether refresh tokens are invalidated on password reset is required (owned by Engineer/System Architect) for security and user experience.
risks: High Risk: Password reset emails are currently not rate-limited, creating a security vulnerability (impact: potentially high, likelihood: confirmed issue).
Risk: Unclarified fallback behavior if API gateway goes down could lead to critical system outages (impact: high, likelihood: unknown until defined, blocking resilience planning).

Risk: Lack of clarity on refresh token invalidation on password reset could introduce security vulnerabilities or inconsistent user experience (impact: medium, likelihood: confirmed gap, blocking complete security design).
milestones: Auth API specification to be published by 2026-07-05.
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset auth endpoints must be completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The system SHALL set JWT token expiry to 24 hours.
The system SHALL implement rate limiting for password reset email requests (inferred — needs confirmation).
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user.
The system SHALL include automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.
data_flows: Authentication requests and responses SHALL flow between the Mobile Application and the Authentication Service via the API Gateway (inferred — needs confirmation).
interface_contracts: The Authentication Service SHALL provide a JWT token with an expiry of 24 hours upon successful login or token refresh (inferred — needs confirmation).
The Authentication Service SHALL expose 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints (inferred — needs confirmation).
The Authentication Service API specification SHALL be published for consumption by dependent teams, specifically the Mobile App team.

constraints.text: The API gateway MUST enforce a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05 to unblock the mobile app team.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior for the system if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
When a user performs a password reset, SHALL their existing refresh tokens be invalidated?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user login and security module has been delivered with no critical issues. However, the mobile app development is currently on hold until the technical details of this module are formally published by July 5th. A significant security risk has been identified regarding password reset emails lacking proper protection.
key_impact: The main user login and security component is stable and ready, ensuring a solid foundation for user access.
There is a current security vulnerability where password reset requests could be abused, requiring urgent attention to implement the agreed-upon limit of 3 attempts per hour.
The mobile application's progress is stalled, impacting the overall project timeline, until the technical specifications for user authentication are finalized and shared.
User experience is balanced with security by setting login session durations to 24 hours.
System stability and security are enhanced through automated testing and limits on how many requests a user can make to our services.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the main communication hub (API gateway) experiences an outage?
When a user changes their password, are all their active login sessions automatically ended for security purposes?

issues.description: The system requirement "The system SHALL implement rate limiting for password reset email requests (inferred — needs confirmation)" incorrectly flags a confirmed requirement as 'inferred — needs confirmation'. Decision D004 explicitly states 'Maximum 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed' and D003 confirms that password reset emails are currently not rate-limited, making the implementation of rate limiting a direct and confirmed requirement.
issues.suggestion: Remove '(inferred — needs confirmation)' and state it as a confirmed system requirement, linking to D003 and D004.

issues.description: The interface contract "The Authentication Service SHALL provide a JWT token with an expiry of 24 hours upon successful login or token refresh (inferred — needs confirmation)" incorrectly flags a confirmed decision as 'inferred — needs confirmation'. Decision D002 explicitly states 'The JWT token expiry duration is set to 24 hours' as a confirmed key decision.
issues.suggestion: Remove '(inferred — needs confirmation)' and state it as a confirmed interface contract, linking to D002.

issues.description: The interface contract "The Authentication Service SHALL expose 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints (inferred — needs confirmation)" incorrectly flags an implicit but confirmed fact as 'inferred — needs confirmation'. Decision D008 states that 'All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests implemented', which confirms the existence and exposure of these endpoints.
issues.suggestion: Remove '(inferred — needs confirmation)' and state it as a confirmed interface contract, linking to D008.

issues.description: The acceptance criteria and test scenarios regarding 'password reset for an unregistered email address' (generic success message, no email) are good security practices but are not explicitly traceable to a specific extracted decision (e.g., D003 or D004 primarily address rate limiting, not this specific scenario).
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a note to the extracted decisions if this was an agreed-upon non-functional requirement, or acknowledge it as a best practice not directly sourced from the provided decisions.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally maintains good quality across most role views, with the PM and Stakeholder views being particularly strong in their targeted communication. However, the Systems Analysis view contains critical issues where several confirmed decisions and their direct implications are incorrectly marked as 'inferred — needs confirmation', misrepresenting the certainty of these facts. There's also a minor info-level issue in the QA view for an un-sourced best practice, but this does not block publishing. The critical issues in the Systems Analysis view require immediate correction.
